title: Add idempotent continuation coverage for verify, commit, and terminal flows
summary: Add the 0.6.0 regression coverage that proves repeated resume and repeated continuation of the same interrupted transaction state are safe across verify, commit, and explicit terminal completion flows. This ticket is the primary idempotence coverage ticket for non-terminal and terminal checkpoints, so the redesigned work loop can tolerate repeated restart, repeated resume entry, and repeated lifecycle handling attempts without corrupting canonical state, duplicating logical completion, minting replacement transaction identities, or collapsing the distinction between commit and terminal success.
outputs: Regression tests for idempotent continuation across verify, commit, and explicit terminal completion flows
Coverage that proves repeated resume of the same interrupted non-terminal state does not corrupt canonical transaction state
Coverage that proves repeated continuation does not mint replacement transaction identities or duplicate logical verify, commit, or terminal completion
Coverage that proves post-commit continuation preserves the non-terminal meaning of `committed` until explicit end occurs
Coverage that proves terminal transactions remain terminal under repeated re-entry and do not become active work again
Documented test expectations for idempotent continuation behavior in the 0.6.0 resumable transaction protocol
acceptance_criteria: Regression coverage exists for repeated resume or repeated continuation of a transaction that is already in a verify-related non-terminal checkpoint.
Regression coverage exists for repeated resume or repeated continuation of a transaction that is already in a commit-related non-terminal checkpoint.
Regression coverage exists for repeated resume or repeated continuation of a transaction that is already in `committed` and still awaiting explicit terminal end handling.
Regression coverage exists for repeated re-entry into transactions that are already terminal as `done` or `blocked`.
The tests prove that repeated continuation of the same interrupted state does not mint a new `tx_id` or otherwise replace the active transaction identity.
The tests prove that repeated continuation does not duplicate logical verify pass, logical commit completion, or explicit terminal completion.
The tests prove that `committed` remains non-terminal in practice and that repeated post-commit continuation still requires explicit terminal handling rather than silently treating commit as success.
The tests prove that terminal transactions are not resumed as active work after repeated resume attempts or re-entry.
The tests prove that canonical transaction state and canonical event history remain consistent under repeated continuation attempts for the same lifecycle checkpoint.

The tests are aligned with the 0.6.0 transaction-centered resumable protocol and do not depend on ticket-derived transaction identity, sentinel active-transaction semantics, or heuristic resume selection.
The regression coverage preserves canonical lifecycle ordering, replay integrity, resumability, idempotent continuation, and explicit terminal-state handling.
tasks: Identify the canonical verify, commit, post-commit, and terminal checkpoint scenarios where repeated resume and repeated continuation must be proven safe.
Add tests for repeated resume or repeated continuation of transactions that are already in verify-related non-terminal states.
Add tests for repeated resume or repeated continuation of transactions that are already in commit-related non-terminal states.
Add tests for repeated resume or repeated continuation of transactions that are already in `committed` and still require explicit `tx.end.done` or equivalent terminal handling.
Add tests for repeated re-entry into transactions that are already terminal as `done` or `blocked`.
Add tests that verify repeated continuation preserves the same exact active transaction identity and does not mint replacement transaction IDs.
Add tests that verify repeated continuation does not duplicate logical verify completion, logical commit completion, or explicit terminal completion.
Add tests that verify canonical state and append-only history remain internally consistent across repeated continuation attempts at the same checkpoint.
Add or update fixtures and helper utilities as needed to simulate repeated restart or repeated resume entry against the same saved canonical state.
Document any bounded assumptions in the tests so future changes do not silently weaken idempotent continuation guarantees.
notes: This ticket is regression-hardening work and should be implemented after the core 0.6.0 runtime semantics and interruption checkpoint coverage are in place.
The most important outcome is confidence that repeated resume or continuation entry against the same saved transaction state is safe and does not corrupt lifecycle meaning.
This is the primary ticket for repeated-resume and idempotent continuation coverage in the 0.6.0 plan.
The tests should focus on logical idempotence at canonical checkpoints rather than brittle implementation-specific call counts unless those counts directly express canonical guarantees.
The tests should make it difficult for future changes to silently collapse `committed` into implicit success or to re-activate terminal transactions.

The tests should reinforce the 0.6.0 design principle that exact active transaction identity plus canonical state plus `next_action` is enough to continue work safely, even when continuation is attempted more than once.

coverage_focus.primary_goal: Prove that repeated resume and repeated continuation of the same interrupted transaction state are safe across verify, commit, and terminal checkpoints.
coverage_focus.non_goal: Treat duplicate low-level internal function execution as inherently invalid when canonical lifecycle meaning remains unchanged and correct.

required_coverage: repeat resume after verify success but before commit completion
repeat resume after commit completion but before explicit terminal end
repeat continuation from the same saved non-terminal checkpoint more than once
idempotent_scenarios.reason: These scenarios validate that the redesigned work loop behaves like a durable resumable transaction protocol under repeated resume and repeated continuation attempts, not only under single clean-path resume.

resume_assertions.exact_identity: Repeated resume or repeated continuation must preserve the same active transaction identity until terminal completion.
resume_assertions.no_duplicate_verify: Repeated resume or repeated continuation must not duplicate logical verify completion.
resume_assertions.no_duplicate_commit: Repeated resume or repeated continuation must not duplicate logical commit completion.
resume_assertions.no_duplicate_terminal_end: Repeated resume or repeated continuation must not duplicate explicit terminal completion.
resume_assertions.non_terminal_commit: `committed` must remain non-terminal until explicit end handling occurs, even under repeated resume or repeated continuation attempts.

state_consistency_assertions.materialized_state: Canonical materialized transaction state must remain internally consistent across repeated continuation attempts.
state_consistency_assertions.event_history: Append-only event history must remain semantically consistent and must not imply duplicated logical lifecycle completion.
state_consistency_assertions.terminal_behavior: Terminal transactions must remain terminal and must not become active resumable work again.

anti_regression_targets: minting replacement transaction IDs during repeat resume
duplicating logical verify pass or verify fail handling
treating committed as implicit done under repeated continuation
re-activating terminal transactions under repeat re-entry

design_bias.preferred_anchor: Checkpoint-oriented idempotence tests that assert exact transaction identity preservation, non-terminal commit semantics, and explicit terminal completion.
avoid: tests that assume a second resume attempt must always be a no-op in every internal detail
tests that blur the distinction between repository commit and terminal success
tests that allow heuristic replacement of active transactions
tests that rely on planning-artifact status as canonical continuation truth

canonical_alignment.synchronization_intent: Use this ticket to lock in repeated-resume and idempotent continuation behavior as durable properties of the 0.6.0 resumable transaction protocol across verify, commit, and terminal flows.
canonical_alignment.current_canonical_constraint: Regression coverage must validate exact active-transaction preservation, non-terminal commit semantics, explicit terminal handling, and repeated-resume safety without reintroducing heuristic recovery or duplicate logical completion.
